Myofibrils are rod-like structures that run the entire length of the muscle fibre, as shown in Figure 2.3a. The number of myofibrils present within a muscle cell can vary but there are thought to be around 2000 in an adult muscle fibre. Myofibrils can be considered as the *contractile apparatus* of the muscle fibre as they contain the contractile proteins actin and myosin. Collectively, actin and myosin comprise 85% of total muscle protein, with myosin taking the lion’s share (60%). The actin protein is 42 kDa in size and is often referred to as the *thin filament*, whereas myosin is referred to as the *thick filament* because it is 480 kDa.

The structure of the thick and thin filaments is shown in Figure 2.4. Note the unique shape of myosin molecules in that each molecule is shaped like two golf clubs twisted together – the myosin tail (i.e. the golf club shafts) points towards the centre of the sarcomere (the *M-line*). The myosin heads (often referred to as the crossbridges) project outward from the shaft in a spiralling fashion and extend towards one of the six thin filaments that surround each thick filament.

## Definition

Myofibrils are rod-like structures that run the entire length of the muscle fibre and serve as the contractile apparatus of the muscle fibre, containing the contractile proteins actin (thin filament) and myosin (thick filament).

## Core Principles

- An adult muscle fibre is thought to contain around 2000 myofibrils, although the number can vary between muscle cells.
- Actin and myosin together comprise 85% of total muscle protein, with myosin contributing the majority share (60%).
- Actin (42 kDa) is referred to as the *thin filament*; myosin (480 kDa) is referred to as the *thick filament*.
- Each myosin molecule is shaped like two golf clubs twisted together: the myosin tail points towards the centre of the sarcomere (the *M-line*), while the myosin heads (crossbridges) project outward from the shaft in a spiralling fashion.
- Each thick filament is surrounded by six thin filaments, and the myosin heads extend towards these surrounding thin filaments.

## Sub-concepts

- **Thin filament (actin):** 42 kDa contractile protein.
- **Thick filament (myosin):** 480 kDa contractile protein, accounting for 60% of total muscle protein.
- **M-line:** the centre of the sarcomere, towards which myosin tails point.
- **Crossbridges:** the myosin heads that project outward from the myosin shaft and interact with the thin filaments.
